Title: Stress-Responsive cis-Regulatory Elements Underline Podophyllotoxin Biosynthesis and Better Performance of Sinopodophyllum hexandrum Under Water Deficit Conditions

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ShPLR encodes PINORESINOL-LARICIRESINOL REDUCTASE, which reduces pinoresinol to secoisolariciresinol in two steps, a dedicated branch toward matairesinol and deoxypodophyllotoxin, the immediate precursor of podophyllotoxin. Its promoter is enriched in MYB-recognizing elements (MYBPLANT, MYB1AT, MYBCORE, ACGTATERD1) and other stress-related motifs, supporting a role in integrating abiotic stress signals into lignan and podophyllotoxin biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ShPLR encodes PINORESINOL-LARICIRESINOL REDUCTASE, a key enzyme converting pinoresinol to secoisolariciresinol in the dedicated branch of podophyllotoxin biosynthesis.
2. A 1,034-bp 5′-upstream promoter of ShPLR was cloned and shown in silico to harbor numerous cis-elements responsive to light, low temperature, dehydration, ABA, and other hormones, particularly multiple MYB/MYC and drought-responsive motifs.
3. ShPLR transcript levels in roots are upregulated under progressive water deficit and remain higher than irrigated controls even after re-watering, in parallel with increased podophyllotoxin accumulation.
4. These data indicate that ShPLR participates in stress-responsive reinforcement of podophyllotoxin biosynthesis, and that its promoter has potential utility as a stress-inducible regulatory element for engineering water-deficit tolerance and lignan/podophyllotoxin production in plants.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Under progressive water deficit, ShPLR is transcriptionally upregulated in roots in parallel with increased podophyllotoxin accumulation, indicating that ShPLR contributes to stress-responsive enhancement of podophyllotoxin biosynthesis in S. hexandrum.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: A 1,034-bp promoter region contains multiple MYB/MYC-binding and ABA/drought-responsive motifs (e.g., MYBPLANT, MYB1AT, MYBCORE, MYCCONSENSUSAT, ACGTATERD1, STRE) as well as light- and temperature-associated cis-elements; ShPLR transcripts are induced by water deficit and decrease upon re-watering, consistent with transcriptional activation via these stress-responsive promoter elements.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Drought/water-deficit and ABA-related stress signaling pathways acting through MYB and MYC family transcription factors and other cis-acting elements to activate the phenylpropanoid and podophyllotoxin biosynthetic pathway.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid-derived lignan and podophyllotoxin biosynthetic pathway; ShPLR catalyzes the two-step reduction of pinoresinol to secoisolariciresinol en route to matairesinol and deoxypodophyllotoxin/podophyllotoxin.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PINORESINOL-LARICIRESINOL REDUCTASE (PLR) enzyme involved in lignan biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In roots, ShPLR transcripts increase rapidly during the initial phase of water deprivation and remain upregulated through 10 and 15 days of stress compared with irrigated controls; expression declines after 15 days of re-watering but remains higher than irrigated plants, and shows an age-dependent gradual increase in both irrigated and stressed plants.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Progressive water deficit induced by withholding irrigation for 5, 10, and 15 days (non-irrigated plants) followed by 15 days of re-watering, compared with plants irrigated every other day with 150 ml water.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Podophyllotoxin biosynthesis and abiotic (water-deficit) stress response in Sinopodophyllum hexandrum.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress response (water-deficit/drought tolerance) and secondary metabolite content (podophyllotoxin accumulation).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Potted S. hexandrum plants collected from Parashar Lake (2,730 m asl, India) and maintained at CSIR-IHBT, Palampur, grown in a controlled growth chamber (25 ± 2 °C, 16 h photoperiod, 200 μmol m^-2 s^-1 light, 70 ± 10% RH); morphologically similar pre-rhizome plants were used, with leaf and root tissues sampled from irrigated and non-irrigated plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: GenomeWalker-based cloning of 5′-upstream genomic sequence; Sanger sequencing; in silico promoter motif prediction using PLACE and PlantCARE; controlled progressive water-deficit and re-watering treatments; physiological and biochemical assays; podophyllotoxin quantification by UPLC; and qRT-PCR expression analysis of ShPLR and other phenylpropanoid/podophyllotoxin pathway genes in roots.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling of ShPLR in roots across 5, 10, and 15 days of water deficit and after 15 days of re-watering, using ShACTIN as internal control, combined with cloning of a 1,034-bp 5′-upstream promoter by GenomeWalker PCR and in silico cis-element analysis (PLACE, PlantCARE).
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length ShPLR coding sequence had been deduced previously; in this study, a 1,034-bp 5′-upstream promoter region was obtained from genomic DNA using GenomeWalker PCR libraries, cloned into pGEM-T-Easy, and Sanger-sequenced.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Promoter of ShPLR contains multiple drought-, temperature-, and light-responsive cis-elements and drives water deficit-inducible expression of a key podophyllotoxin biosynthetic gene, suggesting use of this promoter to engineer stress-inducible expression of transgenes for improved water-deficit tolerance and/or enhanced podophyllotoxin or other lignan production in S. hexandrum or heterologous plant systems.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ShSLD encodes SECOISOLARICIRESINOL DEHYDROGENASE (SLD/SDH), which oxidizes secoisolariciresinol to matairesinol in the podophyllotoxin pathway. Its promoter carries drought-responsive cis-elements including MYB, MYC (CATGTG), ACGTATERD1, and MYBCOREATCYCB1, as well as ARR1/ARR10, light-responsive (SORLIP2AT, GT1CONSENSUS), and ABA-responsive (DPBFCOREDCDC3) motifs, suggesting complex regulation by abiotic stress and hormone signaling.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ShSLD encodes SECOISOLARICIRESINOL DEHYDROGENASE, which converts secoisolariciresinol to matairesinol in the dedicated branch of podophyllotoxin biosynthesis.
2. A 785-bp 5′-upstream promoter region of ShSLD was cloned and shown in silico to contain numerous cis-acting elements responsive to drought/dehydration (MYB, MYC, ACGTATERD1, STRE), ABA (DPBFCOREDCDC3), cytokinin (ARR1, ARR10), and light (GT1CONSENSUS, SORLIP2AT).
3. ShSLD is transcriptionally upregulated in roots during progressive water deficit, and its expression remains higher than in irrigated controls after re-watering, accompanying elevated podophyllotoxin levels.
4. These findings support a role for ShSLD in stress-responsive podophyllotoxin biosynthesis and indicate that its promoter can function as a stress-inducible regulatory element with potential application in engineering drought tolerance and secondary metabolism in plants.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ShSLD transcript abundance in roots increases under water deficit and remains elevated relative to irrigated controls after re-watering, coinciding with enhanced podophyllotoxin accumulation, indicating that ShSLD participates in stress-responsive control of podophyllotoxin biosynthesis in S. hexandrum.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: The 785-bp ShSLD promoter contains multiple abiotic stress-related cis-elements (e.g., MYB, MYC, ACGTATERD1, STRE), ABA-responsive (DPBFCOREDCDC3), cytokinin-related (ARR1, ARR10), and light-responsive (SORLIP2AT, GT1CONSENSUS, RBCSCONSENSUS) motifs; ShSLD is transcriptionally induced by water deficit and partially downregulated upon re-watering, consistent with stress- and hormone-regulated activation via these cis-elements.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Drought/water-deficit, ABA, cytokinin, and light signaling pathways converging on MYB/MYC and other transcription factors that bind the ShSLD promoter to regulate the phenylpropanoid and podophyllotoxin biosynthetic pathway under stress.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid-derived lignan and podophyllotoxin biosynthetic pathway; ShSLD encodes SECOISOLARICIRESINOL DEHYDROGENASE that converts secoisolariciresinol to matairesinol, a direct precursor of deoxypodophyllotoxin and podophyllotoxin.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SECOISOLARICIRESINOL DEHYDROGENASE (SLD/SDH) enzyme involved in lignan and podophyllotoxin biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In roots, ShSLD is upregulated during water deprivation, with transcript levels increasing over 5–15 days of stress in non-irrigated plants relative to irrigated controls; expression decreases after re-watering but remains above control levels, and also shows an age-dependent gradual increase even in the absence of stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Progressive water deficit by withholding irrigation for 5, 10, and 15 days, followed by 15 days of re-watering, compared with plants irrigated every second day with 150 ml water.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of podophyllotoxin biosynthesis and abiotic (water-deficit) stress response in Sinopodophyllum hexandrum.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress response (water-deficit/drought tolerance) and secondary metabolite content (podophyllotoxin accumulation).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Potted Sinopodophyllum hexandrum plants from Parashar Lake (2,730 m asl, India) maintained at CSIR-IHBT, Palampur, grown in a controlled growth chamber (25 ± 2 °C, 16 h photoperiod, 200 μmol m^-2 s^-1 light, 70 ± 10% RH); morphologically uniform pre-rhizome plants subjected to irrigated versus non-irrigated treatments, with root and leaf tissues sampled.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: GenomeWalker-based isolation of a 785-bp 5′-upstream region; Sanger sequencing; in silico promoter motif analysis using PLACE and PlantCARE; controlled water-deficit and re-watering treatments; physiological and biochemical assays; podophyllotoxin quantification; and qRT-PCR expression analysis of ShSLD and related pathway genes in roots.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling of ShSLD in roots across progressive water-deficit (5, 10, 15 days) and recovery, using ShACTIN as reference, together with cloning of a 785-bp 5′-upstream promoter fragment by GenomeWalker PCR and in silico identification of cis-regulatory elements (PLACE, PlantCARE).
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Full-length ShSLD coding sequence had been reported previously; here, a 785-bp 5′-upstream promoter region was obtained from genomic DNA using GenomeWalker PCR libraries, cloned and Sanger-sequenced.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: The ShSLD promoter carries multiple drought-, low-temperature-, light-, and hormone-responsive cis-elements and drives water deficit-inducible expression of a downstream podophyllotoxin biosynthetic gene, supporting its use as a stress-inducible promoter to control transgene expression for improving water-deficit tolerance and modulating podophyllotoxin or related lignan accumulation in S. hexandrum or other crops.
